role_summary: Civil Rights History Project interviewee; the LoC oral history is the primary archival source for this catalog page.
ai_reading: Judy Richardson's top semantic neighbor is Betty Garman Robinson (entry 7, count 172); the broader cluster includes Dorothy "Dottie" Foreman Zellner (#30), Reginald Robinson (#101), Juadine Henderson (#68). The catalog flags a +0.72 reading toward Strategic Vision on the Tactical Pragmatism vs Strategic Vision axis as the most-distinctive structural reading.
biographical_paragraph: Judy Richardson's oral history is held at the Library of Congress as entry 69 of the Civil Rights History Project (American Folklife Center accession AFC 2010/039) [src: 1]. The LoC transcript carries a publication-block audit tier: known issues exist that prevent full publication-grade certification, and any specific biographical or quoted claims drawn from the transcript itself should be confirmed against external sources before citation. Independent biographical sourcing for an extended orientation paragraph is pending; the strongest signal currently available is the embedding pattern named in the AI's reading above.
